description: Universal event ontology shared by ALL games. Game and mode adapters build on top of this. You write this once; every game reuses it.

universal_fields.timestamp: the second of the clip the event happened (a number)
universal_fields.detail: short free-text specifics for the event
universal_fields.confidence: 0.0 to 1.0 - include low-confidence guesses, do not drop them

universal_notes: You are extracting a structured event stream from competitive game footage. Read any on-screen text literally and transcribe it - text is your most reliable signal. Be thorough and OVER-report: include uncertain events with a low confidence (around 0.3) rather than dropping them. Report each discrete event only ONCE, at the timestamp it first appears. A 'match' is one complete game between two players; only mark a match as ended at a clear end-of-match RESULT screen - never from a single unit being eliminated. Return ONLY a JSON array of event objects; return [] only if no gameplay is visible at all.
